title: Kilmaulsi
content:

{{Quote|Squaawk!|A Kilmaulsi thug attacking Dass Jennir|<ref name="DT:BH">[[Dark Times: Blue Harvest, Part 2|''Dark Times: Blue Harvest'', Part 2]]</ref>}}
The '''Kilmaulsi''' were [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Bird/Legends|avians]] indigenous to the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Kilmaulsias]]. While they possessed no wings, they had [[Color/Legends|colorful]] ruffs of feathers under their jaws and along their [[Limb/Legends|arms]]. Members of the species were curious but devout by nature, leading their tribal society to become strongly religious. Originally a warlike species, they ended numerous intertribal conflicts when the [[Vorzydiak]] species made [[Realspace/Legends|space]] travel available to them. Once introduced into the [[The galaxy/Legends|wider galaxy]], the Kilmaulsi became common as guards and [[Soldier/Legends|soldiers]], particularly throughout the [[Vorzyd Cluster]], where their [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] was located. The former [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Dass Jennir]] encountered a [[Unidentified Kilmaulsi thug|Kilmaulsi]] on the planet [[Cato Neimoidia/Legends|Cato Neimoidia]] in [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]], and [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] [[Agent/Legends|agents]] [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]] and [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] ran into a [[Unidentified Kilmaulsi guard|Kilmaulsi]] working as a guard on the world [[Vorzyd V/Legends|Vorzyd V]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]].

==Biology and appearance==
The Kilmaulsi were a [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Species/Legends|species]] of digitigrade [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoids]] descended from [[Bird/Legends|avians]]. While the species had lost their [[Kilmaulsi precursor species|ancestors']] wings and ability to fly, they retained tufts of feathers beneath the jaw and on the elbows. Such plumage was [[Color/Legends|purple]] in at least some members of the species,<ref name="GATORW" /> and black in others. The species' skin, meanwhile, was green, with areas of orange around the [[Eye/Legends|eyes]] and the lower jaw.<ref name="CSW:TEA1" /> Standing at 1.7 [[Meter/Legends|meters]] tall on average, Kilmaulsi were burly and muscular compared to other humanoids, but they shared common humanoid traits, such as a bipedal body structure with two [[Limb/Legends|legs]] and two arms, as well as a torso and a head. The face consisted of two relatively large eyes, a beak-like [[Mouth/Legends|mouth]] with a [[Tongue/Legends|tongue]] inside, and two [[Nose/Legends|nostrils]] above the mouth.<ref name="GATORW" /> The eyes included nictitating membranes<ref name="CSW:TEA1" /> and could be blue<ref name="DT:BH" /> or red in color.<ref name="CSW:TEA1" /> Each arm ended in a hand with four digits, each tipped with a talon, while the legs ended in a long three-digit foot.<ref name="GATORW" />

Kilmaulsi were considered [[Youngling/Legends|children]] until the age of eleven, at which point they were considered young adults until the age of seventeen. Kilmaulsi were at their peak as adults between the ages of eighteen and fifty-five, hitting middle age at fifty-six and being considered old at seventy-five. The species' life expectancy was ninety [[Standard year/Legends|years]] total.<ref name="GATORW" />

==Society and culture==
With a culture forged through war, Kilmaulsi society was split into multiple tribes, each with its own devout religious views. Such differences led to conflict between the tribes, and for much of their history, the Kilmaulsi subgroups were at war. However, the battles were eventually ended by [[Alien/Legends|alien]] contact and the introduction of [[Realspace/Legends|space]] travel. Indeed, the Kilmaulsi became so smitten with traveling the [[Star/Legends|stars]] that they abandoned their age-old rivalries to focus on such voyages.<ref name="GATORW" />

Once the Kilmaulsi were introduced to the rest of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]], their society became more focused on technological advancement, as religion took a back seat as a cultural priority. Despite that change, the Kilmaulsi remained skilled warriors, valued as [[Soldier/Legends|soldiers]] by the other sentient species who inhabited the [[Vorzyd Cluster]]: the [[Hrakian]]s, [[Paigun]]s, [[Selonian/Legends|Selonians]], and [[Vorzydiak]]s. While in combat, Kilmausi warriors typically wore ornate green armor that was similar in all technical respects to battle armor, though at least one individual sported a purple version. While fighting, they could enter a battle frenzy that made them appear as colorful blurs due to their speed and prowess. While in frenzy, however, Kilmaulsi could not focus on precise tasks.<ref name="GATORW" />

Individual Kilmaulsi were devout, curious, and resolute, traits that gave rise to the strongly religious period in their history and the obsession with exploration that followed it. Members of the species relished contact with other more peaceful species, although non-Kilmaulsi often found that the avians lacked a sense of humor. Kilmaulsi were capable of speaking [[Galactic Basic Standard/Legends|Galactic Basic Standard]] and so could converse with other species, but amongst themselves, they spoke their own [[Language/Legends|language]], which was also named [[Kilmaulsi (language)|Kilmaulsi]]. The language had a writing system as well and could be learned by non-Kilmaulsi such as [[Sanglui]], a Squalris [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] leader; and [[Hathox Greb]], a Hrakian pro-[[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] civil [[Captain/Legends|captain]].<ref name="GATORW" /> At least one Kilmaulsi also produced squawking noises whilst fighting.<ref name="DT:BH" /> Examples of Kilmalusi names were Aryllan, Dawre, Eshalak, Lykern, Kir, Prakk, Thilvera, and Zarlia.<ref name="GATORW" />

==History==
{{Quote|My apologies citizens! Patrons are not allowed mechanical assistance at the games! Your droids must remain outside!|A Kilmaulsi guard on Vorzyd V informs Luke Skywalker and Leia Organa that their droids are not allowed inside the Casino Royale|<ref name="GW" />}}
The Kilmaulsi evolved on the [[Tree/Legends|forest]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of [[Kilmaulsias]] in the [[Kilmauls system/Legends|Kilmauls system]] of the [[Vorzyd Cluster]]. Their early history on their [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] consisted of numerous conflicts between rival tribes based on religious differences. These wars abruptly ended upon the arrival of [[Scout/Legends|explorers]] from the Vorzydiak species, who had recently acquired [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]] technology themselves after explorers from the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] discovered them.<ref name="GATORW" /> Indeed, the area around the Vorzyd Cluster was widely explored between [[5000 BBY/Legends|5000]]<ref name="EA">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref> The Vorzydiaks, like the Kilmaulsi, originated from within the Vorzyd Cluster, and they peacefully established contact with each of the warring Kilmaulsi tribes and introduced them to the concept of space travel. Alien contact had a profound effect on the Kilmaulsi, prompting them to end their bitter rivalries and leading many to migrate off-world to seek their fortunes among the stars.<ref name="GATORW" />

[[File:Kilmaulsi_guard.jpg|thumb|left|upright|Many Kilmaulsi worked as guards at the Casino Royale.]]
Within a [[Standard century/Legends|century]] of the area's discovery, the species of the Vorzyd Cluster—including the Kilmaulsi, Paiguns, Hrakians, Vorzydiaks, and a lost colony of Selonians—formed a loose confederacy from their individual holdings, a government known as the [[Commonality]]. Hundreds of Kilmaulsi were hired to work as guards at the newly built [[Casino Royale]], a [[Casino/Legends|gambling establishment]] on the world of [[Vorzyd V/Legends|Vorzyd V]]. The guards wore armor on their thoraxes and were always extraordinarily polite to guests, but kept undesirables out of the casino including [[Droid/Legends|droids]], with their intimidating appearances normally enough to scare the unwanted arrivals away. During this [[Time/Legends|time]], a [[Voryzd V Kilmaulsi embassy|Kilmaulsi embassy]] was also constructed in [[Efavan/Legends|Efavan]], the [[Capital/Legends|capital]] [[City/Legends|city]] of Vorzyd V where the Casino was located.<ref name="GATORW" />

{{RPGseed}}
After the fall of the Empire as a [[Galaxy/Legends|galactic]] power, many Kilmaulsi on Vorzyd V became disillusioned and were hired by [[Quaffug]] the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]], a criminal intent on forcing the Casino Royale to cut him a share of their profits. Concerned with conflict on Vorzyd V affecting tourism, the [[Governor/Legends|governors]] of the Casino Royale hired a number of [[Spacer/Legends|spacers]] to persuade or force Quaffug into leaving using non-violent methods.<ref name="GATORW" />
{{Endgame}}

==Kilmaulsi in the galaxy==
{{Quote|Aawwk!|A Kilmaulsi thug, when attacked by Dass Jennir|<ref name="DT:BH" />}}
[[File:Jennir_faces_Kilmaulsi.jpg|thumb|right|upright|Former Jedi Dass Jennir fought a Kilmaulsi on Cato Neimoidia.]]
After their introduction to space travel, the Kilmaulsi species took to the practice quickly, and many left their homeworld to explore the galaxy and seek their fortune off-world. A large number of Kilmaulsi booked passage on container [[Starship/Legends|ships]] from the nearby world of [[Hraki]] and found employment as laborers and soldiers elsewhere. After migration began, Kilmaulsi became a common sight throughout the cluster, usually serving in military roles or as manual labor.<ref name="GATORW" /> Kilmailsi could be found traveling away from their homeworld as early as [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]] when, four [[Standard month/Legends|months]] after [[Order 66/Legends|Order 66]] had taken place, a [[Unidentified Kilmaulsi thug|Kilmaulsi]] was present on the [[Neimoidian/Legends|Neimoidian]] [[Purse world/Legends|purse world]] of [[Cato Neimoidia/Legends|Cato Neimoidia]]. The Kilmaulsi was affiliated with a [[Unidentified Chistori thug|member]] of the [[Reptile/Legends|reptilian]] [[Chistori]] species, a cohort for whom the avian agreed to help fight off rivals seeking the same employment. [[Ember Chankeli]], a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|woman]] from the planet [[Telerath/Legends|Telerath]], was offering the job, which required those hired to remove a [[Musori's gang|gang]] of [[Slavery/Legends|enslavers]] from her hometown. When the Chistori and the Kilmaulsi—along with several Neimoidian associates—arrived to accept the job, however, a pair of other candidates was already there, one of whom was the Human former Jedi [[Dass Jennir]], with whom the Chistori had previously fought for the job. The Chistori and his friends attacked Jennir, only to be defeated quickly in combat by Jennir's subtle use of [[The Force/Legends|the Force]].<ref name="DT:BH" />

During the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], when the [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] and [[Princess/Legends|Princess]] [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]] of [[Alderaan/Legends|Alderaan]]—both Human agents of the Alliance to Restore the Republic—visited Vorzyd V to disrupt the Galactic Empire's gambling operations there, they encountered a [[Unidentified Kilmaulsi guard|Kilmaulsi guard]] at the Casino Royale. The guard informed them that their droids, [[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]] and [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]], would be unable to enter with them because mechanical assistance was not allowed.<ref name="GW" />

==Behind the scenes==
A Kilmaulsi first appeared in the [[Star Wars (comic strip)|comic strip]] ''[[Gambler's World]]'', written and illustrated by [[Russ Manning]]. The strip was distributed between [[March 12]] and [[September 8]], [[1979]], by the [[Los Angeles Times Syndicate]] in American and Canadian newspapers. However, the strip gave no name or background information for the species.<ref name="GW" /> The story was colorized and reprinted in [[1994]] by [[Dark Horse Comics]] as part of the [[Star Wars comic books|comic book]] [[Classic Star Wars: The Early Adventures 1|''Classic Star Wars: The Early Adventures'' 1]]. The colors, by [[Ray Murtaugh]], revealed the skin of the Kilmaulsi to be green.<ref name="CSW:TEA1" />

The species did not appear again until [[2004]], when they were named and detailed as a species in the [[Wizards of the Coast]] ''[[Star Wars Roleplaying Game]]'' sourcebook ''[[Geonosis and the Outer Rim Worlds]]'', written by several authors. The publication also included an image of a Kilmaulsi by [[Mark Tedin]]. The book included [[Roleplaying game|roleplaying]] stats for a Kilmaulsi commoner, giving them average scores for each of the different attributes.<ref name="GATORW" /> ''Gamblers World'' was then republished, in color again, as webstrips on [[Hyperspace: The Official Star Wars Fan Club|Hyperspace]], a premium section of the [[StarWars.com]] website.<ref name="HS">"[[Gambler's World]]"</ref> In [[2009]], another Kilmaulsi appeared, this time in ''[[Dark Times: Blue Harvest, Part 2]]'', the second of two mini-issues of Dark Horse Comics' ''[[Star Wars: Dark Times]]'' comic book series, which was designed as an introduction to the [[Star Wars: Dark Times: Blue Harvest|''Blue Harvest'']] story arc.<ref name="DT:BH" /> The issue was written by [[Randy Stradley]],<ref name="Blaster Star Wars Insider">{{InsiderCite|127|Blaster}}</ref> under the name "Mike Harrison," and was illustrated by [[Douglas Wheatley]].<ref name="DT:BH" />
